info.name: kgrag
info.description:
End-to-end flow with FULL request bodies (every field, required + optional, with sample values).

Flow: register user → login → create company → create project → ingest code → ingest documents (Brain) → search.

Usage:
1. Import this collection AND the matching environment (kgrag.postman_environment.json).
2. Select the `kgrag-local` environment.
3. Edit environment variables (user_email, user_password, company_name, ...) to taste.
4. Run the requests IN ORDER — each `create` request auto-saves the returned id into the environment.

Servers (local Docker stack):
- Auth:     http://localhost:8001
- REST API: http://localhost:8000

Authentication: Login stores `access_token` into the environment. Collection-level Bearer auth uses `{{access_token}}` for every request after Login. Health checks, /register and /login override with `noauth`.

request.description:
Creates a new user.

Schema (`auth.UserCreate`):
- `email`    EmailStr  REQUIRED
- `username` str       REQUIRED (3-50 chars)
- `password` str       REQUIRED (min 8 chars)
- `roles`    list[str] optional (default `["user"]`; allowed values include `"user"`, `"admin"`)

Response: `UserResponse` (id, email, username, roles, is_active, is_superuser, created_at, profile fields).
Test script saves `user_id`.

Exchanges credentials for tokens.

Schema (`auth.UserLogin`):
- `email`    EmailStr REQUIRED
- `password` str      REQUIRED

Response: `TokenResponse` (`access_token`, `refresh_token`, `token_type="bearer"`, `expires_in`).
If the account has 2FA enabled, response is `TwoFactorChallengeResponse` (`requires_2fa=true`, `challenge_token`) — complete via POST /login/2fa.

Test script saves `access_token` and `refresh_token` to the environment.

Exchanges a refresh token for a new access token.

Field:
- `refresh_token`  str  REQUIRED (returned by /login)

Response: `TokenResponse`. Test script overwrites `access_token`/`refresh_token` in the environment.
Returns the current authenticated user (id, email, username, roles, companies, is_active, is_superuser, profile fields). Sanity check for the JWT.

Creates a new company; the current user becomes its owner.

Schema (`auth.CompanyCreate`):
- `name`  str  REQUIRED (1-200 chars)

Response: `CompanyResponse` (`id`, `name`, `created_at`, `is_active`, `user_count`, `cognee_enabled`).
Test script saves `company_id`.
Lists every company the current user belongs to.

Creates a project under a company with full instructions filled in.

Schema (`api.models.project.ProjectCreate`):
- `name`         str  REQUIRED (1-255 chars). NOTE: search endpoints slugify this when used as the project scope (e.g. `vet_ui` → `vet-ui`).
- `description`  str  optional
- `instructions` ProjectInstructionsCreate  optional. ALL sub-fields optional:
  - `description`         str        — what the project is
  - `languages`           list[str]  — programming languages used
  - `frameworks`          list[str]  — frameworks used
  - `tools`               list[str]  — tools and infrastructure
  - `architecture_notes`  str        — key architectural decisions
  - `conventions`         str        — project-specific patterns
  - `custom_instructions` str        — anything else

Response: `ProjectResponse` (id, company_id, name, description, webhook_url, github_webhook_secret, github_token_set, created_at, updated_at, instructions{}).
Test script saves `project_id`.
Same endpoint as the FULL variant — shows the minimum body. `description` and `instructions` are optional.

Lists all projects in the given company.

GET /api/v1/companies/{company_id}/projects → `ProjectListResponse { projects: [...], total: int }`.

Returns `ProjectResponse`:
- `id`, `company_id`, `name`, `description`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
- `webhook_url` — URL configured for GitHub webhooks (e.g. `http://<host>:8000/api/v1/webhooks/github/{company_name}/{project_name}`)
- `github_webhook_secret` — MASKED preview (full value only shown once via Regenerate)
- `github_token_set` — `bool` indicating if a PAT is configured for this project. The token itself is NEVER returned.
- `instructions` — embedded `ProjectInstructionsEmbedded` object

body.raw:
{
  "name": "{{project_name}}",
  "description": "Updated description",
  "github_token": "ghp_yourpersonalaccesstoken",
  "instructions": {
    "description": "Updated project description",
    "languages": ["python"],
    "frameworks": ["fastapi"],
    "tools": ["postgres", "qdrant", "neo4j"],
    "architecture_notes": "Updated notes",
    "conventions": "Updated conventions",
    "custom_instructions": "Updated extras"
  }
}

request.description:
Updates a project. Method is **PUT** (per `projects_v2.py:146`). Schema (`api.models.project.ProjectUpdate`) — ALL fields optional, send any subset; COALESCE semantics (omitted fields keep current values):
- `name`         str | null
- `description`  str | null
- `github_token` str | null — per-project GitHub PAT for cloning private repos. Stored in `projects.github_token`. Falls back to env `GITHUB_TOKEN` if unset. NEVER returned in responses (only `github_token_set: bool`).
- `instructions` ProjectInstructionsCreate | null — upserted if provided.

Returns updated `ProjectResponse`.

Generates a fresh HMAC-SHA256 secret used to authenticate GitHub push webhooks for this project.

**⚠ The full secret is returned ONLY ONCE in this response.** After this call, GET /projects/{id} only returns a masked preview.

Response: `WebhookSecretResponse`:
- `project_id`            str
- `webhook_url`           str — full URL to put in GitHub's webhook config (`http://<host>:8000/api/v1/webhooks/github/{company_name}/{project_name}`)
- `github_webhook_secret` str — the new secret (copy now)
- `message`               str

Use when: setting up a new webhook integration, rotating after suspected compromise, or for security hygiene.

After regenerating, configure your GitHub repo:
- Webhook URL: the `webhook_url` from response
- Content type: `application/json`
- Secret: the `github_webhook_secret` from response
- Events: push (and optionally `pull_request`, `create`)
- Active: ✔

Transfers a project and ALL project-scoped data to another company.

Schema (`ProjectTransferRequest`):
- `target_company_id` str  REQUIRED — UUID of the destination company

Migrates ownership/scope across:
- PostgreSQL (project row + repositories + branches + instructions + engagements + tasks)
- Neo4j (every node with `project_id`)
- Qdrant (every point with `project_id` metadata)
- MongoDB ingestions (legacy table — may be a no-op if mongodb is not deployed)

Includes validation, authorization, idempotency checks, and a best-effort compensation attempt if PostgreSQL transfer fails after the external moves.

Response: `ProjectTransferResponse` with per-database status.

**⚠ DESTRUCTIVE — cannot be undone.** Deletes the project and everything scoped to it:

- PostgreSQL: `projects` row (cascades to repositories, branches, engagements, tasks, task_artifacts, project_instructions)
- Neo4j: every node with this `project_id` (Project, Repository, Branch, Document, Code, Entity, Knowledge, Expertise, Fact)
- Qdrant: every vector with this `project_id` in metadata
- Local filesystem: cloned git repositories under `rag_storage/repos`

Returns deletion statistics per database.

Publishes a `RepositoryIngestionRequest` to Kafka topic `incoming_requests`. Returns `202 Accepted` immediately — ingestion runs asynchronously.

Schema (`api.routes.ingestion.RepositoryRequest`):
- `repository`          str   REQUIRED (1+ chars). Typically `owner/name`; the worker resolves it to a clone URL.
- `branch`              str   REQUIRED (1+ chars). e.g. `main`, `develop`.
- `framework`           str   REQUIRED (1+ chars). Classifier hint: `python`, `node`, `rails`, `go`, ...
- `project_id`          str   REQUIRED (UUID). Must reference an existing project the JWT user has access to.
- `force_full_refresh`  bool  optional (default `false`). When `true`, every file is re-processed regardless of git diff.

GitHub auth resolution (in order):
1. `projects.github_token` for this `project_id` (set via PATCH /api/v1/projects/{id}).
2. env `GITHUB_TOKEN` (global fallback).
3. no token (public repos only).

Downstream: code-preprocessor → entity-extraction + summarization + qdrant-storage (parallel) → embedding → qdrant-storage + neo4j-storage.

Response:
```json
{
  "status": "queued",
  "repository": "...",
  "project_id": "...",
  "company_id": "...",
  "topic": "incoming_requests",
  "branch": "...",
  "framework": "...",
  "force_full_refresh": false
}
```
Same as the FULL variant but with `force_full_refresh=true`. Use after changing chunker/prompts/schemas or to reprocess a stale dataset.

Adds a KNOWLEDGE item.

Proto schema (`kgrag.brain.AddToBrainRequest`):
- `kind`        enum  REQUIRED — `KNOWLEDGE` (2) | `EXPERTISE` (1) | `LESSON` (3). REST accepts the string name.
- `title`       str   REQUIRED
- `content`     str   REQUIRED — Markdown text as a string (NO file upload).
- `metadata`    map<str,str> optional — arbitrary tagging.
- `when_to_use` str   optional — EXPERTISE-only. Ignored for KNOWLEDGE/LESSON.
- `symptom`     str   optional — LESSON-only.
- `root_cause`  str   optional — LESSON-only.
- `solution`    str   optional — LESSON-only.
- `prevention`  str   optional — LESSON-only.
- `severity`    str   optional — LESSON-only. `"low"` | `"medium"` | `"high"` | `"critical"` | `""`.

Flow: REST mints Cognee JWT → `BrainContentService.AddToBrain` on `kgrag-cognee:50052` → Postgres write + Kafka `brain_events` → document-preprocessor chunks/embeds → `enriched-code-chunks` → entity-extraction + summarization → embedding → Qdrant `{company_id}_knowledge` + Neo4j `cognee-{company_id}`.

Scope = company derived from JWT. `company_id` is never client-supplied.

Test script saves `brain_content_id`.

body.raw:
{
  "kind": "EXPERTISE",
  "title": "Diagnosing Neo4j auth failures",
  "content": "# Neo4j auth failure playbook

If you see `WARN The client is unauthorized due to authentication failure` repeatedly:

1. The volume was initialised with a different password than what's now in .env.
2. Either reset the volume (dev) or change the env to match (prod).

## Reset volume
```bash
docker compose stop neo4j
docker volume rm <project>_neo4j_data
docker compose up -d neo4j
```",
  "when_to_use": "Apply when Neo4j logs show repeated bolt-auth-failure warnings on startup.",
  "metadata": {
    "area": "infra",
    "component": "neo4j"
  }
}

request.description:
Adds an EXPERTISE item. EXPERTISE-specific extras:
- `when_to_use` — short hint about when this expertise should be applied. Used during retrieval scoring.

LESSON-specific fields (`symptom`, `root_cause`, `solution`, `prevention`, `severity`) are IGNORED for EXPERTISE.

item.name: Add LESSON (FULL — every field, full postmortem)

body.raw:
{
  "kind": "LESSON",
  "title": "REST API container couldn't read .env when compose ran from subdir",
  "content": "# Symptom\nkgrag-rest-api container had `GEMINI_API_KEY=` (empty) even though .env contained the key.\n\n# Fix\nAdded `--env-file ./.env` to every docker compose invocation via the Makefile.\n\n# Why this happened\nDocker compose resolves `.env` relative to the compose file's directory (or CWD), not the repo root. Our compose lives at `docker/docker-compose.yml`, so without `--env-file` it looked for `docker/.env`.",
  "symptom": "Empty env vars in container despite values being present in .env.",
  "root_cause": "docker compose looks for .env next to the compose file, not the repo root.",
  "solution": "Pass --env-file <path/to/.env> on every compose invocation; bake it into the Makefile.",
  "prevention": "Add a Makefile-only invocation pattern; document the requirement in SETUP.md.",
  "severity": "medium",
  "metadata": {
    "area": "infra",
    "component": "docker-compose",
    "detected_in": "Phase 4 dev run"
  }
}

request.description:
Adds a LESSON item with full postmortem fields.

LESSON-specific extras (all optional, recommended for incident records):
- `symptom`     str — what was observed
- `root_cause`  str — underlying reason
- `solution`    str — how it was fixed
- `prevention`  str — how to avoid recurrence
- `severity`    str — `"low"` | `"medium"` | `"high"` | `"critical"` | `""`

`when_to_use` is IGNORED for LESSON.

query.description: REQUIRED. One of KNOWLEDGE | EXPERTISE | LESSON.

request.description: Lists brain items of a single `kind` for the company derived from the JWT. Paginated.

query.description: REQUIRED. Must match the kind it was created with.

request.description: Get a single brain content row. `kind` query param is required (tables are kind-partitioned).

Updates a brain item. `kind` is REQUIRED and must match the kind at creation. `title` and `content` are optional (omit to keep current). `metadata` replaces existing metadata when sent. Kind-specific fields irrelevant to the row's kind are ignored.

query.description: REQUIRED. Must match the kind it was created with.

request.description: Deletes a brain content row.

body.raw:
{
  "query": "how is JWT validated on incoming requests",
  "project_id": "{{project_id}}",
  "project_name": "{{project_name}}",
  "search_type": "CHUNKS_LEXICAL",
  "limit": 10,
  "depth": "thorough",
  "system_prompt": "",
  "top_k": 0,
  "wide_search_top_k": 0
}

request.description:
Project-scoped code search via Cognee. Routing: REST → mints Cognee JWT → gRPC `CogneeService.FlexibleCodeSearch` on `kgrag-cognee:50052`.

Fields:
- `query`               str   REQUIRED  — search question
- `project_id`          UUID  REQUIRED  — project to scope to
- `project_name`        str   REQUIRED  — namespace anchor. Server slugifies (`vet_ui` → `vet-ui`).
- `search_type`         str   optional, default `"CHUNKS_LEXICAL"`. Allowed:
    `"CHUNKS"`, `"CHUNKS_LEXICAL"`, `"RAG_COMPLETION"`, `"SUMMARIES"`,
    `"GRAPH_COMPLETION"`, `"GRAPH_SUMMARY_COMPLETION"`,
    `"GRAPH_COMPLETION_COT"`, `"GRAPH_COMPLETION_CONTEXT_EXTENSION"`,
    `"FEELING_LUCKY"`, `"TEMPORAL"`, `"TRIPLET_COMPLETION"`.
- `limit`               int   optional, 1-100, default 10
- `depth`               str   optional, `"concise"` | `"standard"` | `"thorough"` (default `"thorough"`). Picks `system_prompt` + `top_k` presets.
- `system_prompt`       str   optional. Raw override; takes precedence over `depth`. Empty string = use depth preset.
- `top_k`               int   optional, default 0 (= use depth preset). Number of vector hits.
- `wide_search_top_k`   int   optional, default 0 (= use depth preset). Wider candidate pool for fusion.

`company_id` is derived from JWT — never client-supplied.

body.raw:
{
  "query": "what does the authenticate function depend on",
  "project_id": "{{project_id}}",
  "project_name": "{{project_name}}",
  "search_type": "GRAPH_COMPLETION",
  "limit": 5,
  "depth": "standard"
}

request.description: Same endpoint as 'Search code (FULL)' but `search_type=GRAPH_COMPLETION` — walks the code call graph from query-anchored entities + LLM completion. Best for impact / dependency questions.
`search_type=CHUNKS` — pure vector similarity over code chunks. Best for fuzzy semantic matching where keyword overlap is low.

body.raw:
{
  "query": "how do I fix neo4j auth failures",
  "search_type": "CHUNKS",
  "limit": 10,
  "depth": "thorough",
  "system_prompt": "",
  "top_k": 0,
  "wide_search_top_k": 0
}

request.description:
Hybrid semantic search over the company's brain (KNOWLEDGE + EXPERTISE + LESSON). Routing: REST → mints Cognee JWT → gRPC `CogneeService.FlexibleCogneeSearch` on `kgrag-cognee:50052`.

Fields:
- `query`               str   REQUIRED
- `search_type`         str   optional, default `"CHUNKS"`. Same enum as code search.
- `limit`               int   optional, 1-100, default 10
- `depth`               str   optional, `"concise"` | `"standard"` | `"thorough"` (default `"thorough"`).
- `system_prompt`       str   optional. Raw override; takes precedence over `depth`.
- `top_k`               int   optional, default 0.
- `wide_search_top_k`   int   optional, default 0.

Scope = company derived from JWT. No client-supplied `company_id` / `project_id`.
Same endpoint, `search_type=GRAPH_COMPLETION` — graph traversal + LLM synthesis. Best for questions whose answer spans multiple brain items.
